_note: Manual overrides for GA legislative race candidates, applied after build_legislative_races.py generates from source data. Keys are candidate IDs (e.g. 'ga-house-1-2026-r-1'). Use _name for human-readability only — it is stripped before merging. Fields here override the auto-generated values. Incumbents get imageUrl auto-populated from ga-members.json; use this file to correct bad source data or add fields for challengers.

_raceOverrides._note: Race-level field overrides keyed by race ID. Applied by build_legislative_races.py after building each race. Use '_note' to set the Open Seat banner text (format: 'Open seat — Reason.').

ga-house-1-2026-r-1.bio: In 2020 Mike was elected as State Representative for Georgia House District 1, which includes parts of Walker and all of Dade Counties. In 2022 and 2024, Mike was reelected as your State Representative.

ga-house-113-2026-d-2.bio: As your State Representative, I will be a progressive voice in the Georgia State House and a present, accountable partner here at home. I will work to lower the cost of living, expand access to quality and affordable healthcare, strengthen our public schools, and hold powerful corporations accountable when they put profits over people. You will see me at the grocery store, the park, church, neighborhood meetings, and local cookouts; listening, learning, and serving. Representation does not stop in Atlanta; it begins and ends in the community. In the spirit of my father's legacy, and guided by the values he passed down to me, I am proud to announce my candidacy for the Georgia House of Representatives in District 113.

ga-house-113-2026-d-1.bio: I have served as a local elected official where I have written resolutions and championed community priorities. As a lobbyist for the State of Georgia, I have written bills and testified on various issues affecting our communities. My work with Newton County Commission demonstrated my commitment to delivering results and understanding how to navigate the legislative process effectively. I believe in concrete action and will consistently report on the progress and outcomes of my efforts, ensuring transparency and trust.

ga-house-114-2026-d-1.bio: I am Michael Caw, a retired research and development technician, community volunteer, and proud resident of Newborn, Georgia. I am running for the Georgia House of Representatives to bring thoughtful, data-driven leadership and a neighbor's common sense to the State Capitol.

ga-house-114-2026-r-1.bio: Georgia might be ranked the number one state to do business, and that's great if you're a large corporation, but I'm focused on making Georgia the best place in the country to own and grow a small business. That means cutting red tape, making essential agencies more efficient, and pushing bold initiatives like eliminating the state income tax. Now is not the time for conservatives to be timid. We need leaders in Atlanta with real world experience who will fight for working families and main street—not just the big guys.

ga-house-115-2026-r-1.bio: I'm running to ensure that responsible growth in our communities benefits residents, not just developers; that homeowners aren't priced out of their own neighborhoods; that our infrastructure, schools, and public safety measures keep pace with the growth; and that the needs of seniors and working families are respected and not overlooked. I believe that government should be accountable, practical, and deeply rooted in the real lives of the people it represents. My goal is straightforward: to be a voice you recognize, a leader you can reach, and a representative who shows up and gets the important work done.

ga-house-115-2026-d-1.bio: Elected to the Georgia General Assembly in 2020, Lewis-Ward partnered with the Gordon State College Foundation to establish an empowerment scholarship for women interested in American politics, aiming to address the under-representation of African American women in public office. In 2025, she established a second scholarship at Clayton State University to inspire future leaders in public service. She also secured a grant through a program administered by the GA Forestry Commission to increase urban tree canopy, directing resources to disadvantaged communities across her legislative district. Lewis-Ward helped secure almost $400,000 in the FY 2022 state budget for students in her district to participate in the American Association of Adapted Sports Program, and $39,000 for the startup of a Court Appointed Special Advocate (CASA) program in Henry County, GA. She also co-sponsored bipartisan legislation, 2021 HB 676, now signed into law, that provides legislative advisory oversight of state farmers' markets. In 2023, the House of Representatives passed HB 144, the George Burgess Act, introduced by Representative Lewis-Ward to strengthen the rights of adult wards under guardianship.

ga-senate-42-2026-r-1.bio: Grounded in faith, guided by the Constitution, and driven by a commitment to serve others, Bret is ready to lead with bold conservative leadership. He will bring the same discipline, grit, and determination he learned in the Marine Corps to the fight for faith, family, and freedom. Our families do not need more politics as usual, we need a conservative fighter. Bret Dunn is ready to bring bold, principled and unapologetic conservative leadership to the Senate. He will stand tall to fight for our values and deliver real conservative results.

ga-senate-43-2026-d-2.bio: Formerly House Representative from District 113, she was suspended from her seat by Gov. Kemp due to her ongoing criminal case.

ga-senate-43-2026-d-3.bio: Senator Tonya P. Anderson, a Democrat, has served in the Georgia State Senate since 2016. She represents Senate District 43, which includes Rockdale County and portions of Dekalb, Gwinnett and Newton counties. Prior to serving as State Senator, she served as the 1st State Representative for House District 92. Prior to serving as a State Representative, she served as City Councilwoman and Mayor for the City of Lithonia.

ga-house-118-2026-d-1.bio: I'm a Newton County homeowner, a mother, and a small business owner since 2012. I built my career by solving real problems for real people—helping organizations grow, leading strategy, and delivering outcomes. Now, I'm bringing that same results-driven approach to the State House—focused on what families in District 118 are living every day: cost of living pressures, school and youth outcomes, public safety, and healthcare.

ga-house-118-2026-d-2.bio: I'm stepping forward not just as a candidate—but as someone who believes in the power of change. For too long, many in our communities have felt unheard and unseen. I'm stepping forward because I believe our district deserves leadership that uplifts families, strengthens, protects our rights and stands firmly for justice for all. This moment is about rising together. It's about turning our challenges into purpose and our hopes into real progress for the people who call District 118 home. When we stand together, we can build a future where every voice matters and every family has the opportunity to thrive. I'm honored to take this step with you.

ga-house-118-2026-r-1.bio: State Representative Clint Crowe represents the citizens of House District 118, which includes Butts County, as well as portions of Monroe and Newton counties. He was elected to the Georgia House of Representatives in 2020. Rep. Crowe is a second-generation realtor, and he has worked in the real estate industry for more than 20 years. Prior to running a real estate business, Rep. Crowe had a decade long career in law enforcement as a patrolman/field training officer and detective with a metro Atlanta police department.

ga-senate-11-2026-r-1.bio: In the State Senate, Sam will continue to modernize tax policies, improve incentives and remove outdated codes that create financial roadblocks for our local economies. He will continue to educate our legislators on the workforce challenges that rural Georgia faces and the regulations that are stifling our ability to thrive - from EPA to labor and water.

ga-senate-10-2026-d-2.bio: Katherine “Kate” Denny is a candidate for Georgia’s Senate District 10 in the 2026 General Election. With a platform focused on healthcare, education/childcare, wages, and housing, Kate brings nearly two decades of medical experience, having been a trauma nurse for nearly 20 years, and a lived understanding of the challenges facing working families across Georgia.

ga-senate-10-2026-d-4.name: Sylvester Smith III
ga-senate-10-2026-d-4.bio: Sylvester Smith is a father, community leader, data specialist, and lifelong advocate for District 10. He has lived, raised a family, and served across the district for decades. He has led civic organizations, strengthened youth programs, and represented District 10’s values at the local, county, and state levels. District 10 deserves safe communities, strong schools, revitalized corridors, and opportunities for every family. I’m running for State Senate to bring disciplined leadership, strategic thinking, and community-focused solutions to the Capitol.

ga-senate-10-2026-d-3.bio: Because of his background and efforts in the field of public health, The Honorable Larry Johnson was appointed in 2005 to chair the Health Disparities Subcommittee of the Health Steering Committee for NACo, the National Association of Counties. In 2008, he was appointed as Vice Chair of the NACo Health Steering Committee. The Health Steering Committee has jurisdiction, on a national policy level, over all matters pertaining to health care delivery and financing including: indigent care health, care for the uninsured, Medicaid, Medicare, long-term care, local public health programs, mental health, substance abuse and development disabilities.

ga-senate-10-2026-d-1.bio: A tireless champion for underserved communities, Gabrielle has directed over $3 million in resources and led initiatives that have impacted more than 60,000 lives. Her leadership has earned recognition from the Atlanta Business Chronicle’s 40 Under Forty, DeKalb County School District’s Difference Maker Award, Zeta Phi Beta’s Community Pillar Award, and the Georgia Federation of Business and Professional Women’s Young Professional of the Year.|

ga-senate-10-2026-r-1.bio: Running for GA Senate District 10 as a Republican.

ga-house-7-2026-r-1.name: Johnny Chastain
ga-house-7-2026-r-1.bio: I am tired of politicians that, while running, take positions on issues, make promises and statements [that they end up later breaking] and run a slash and burn campaign attacking their opponents. That's not me.  I won't do it.  I will, however, tell you where my core values come from, and how I will make decisions. Conservative Christian values are at the core of who I am - this means that I am pro-life and will work to protect the innocent & unborn. Constitution Conservative: what does our U.S. and Georgia Constitution say?  Words have meaning.  Our forefathers, with wisdom from the Almighty, wrote documents that have lasted the ages enshrining our Rights.  The government did not give, nor can they take our Rights away. Fiscal Conservative: I will lead on the budget to give you and your family your dollars back  and eliminate the State Income Tax. Per capita spending in Georgia is lowest in the country. I will work to spend wisely and balance the budget each year.

ga-house-22-2026-r-1.bio: Jordan Ridley is a proud Georgia conservative, lifelong Cherokee County resident, and a proven Republican leader who has dedicated his life to serving his community and defending the values that make Georgia strong. In 2024, Jordan served as a local advocate for President Donald Trump’s re-election in Georgia, speaking with both grassroots groups and the national media—including The New York Times—about President Trump’s record, grassroots support, and the high stakes facing Georgia and the country in the 2024 election.

ga-house-23-2026-r-1.name: Bill Fincher
ga-house-23-2026-r-1.bio: I have spent my career upholding the law, managing budgets, and serving the people of Georgia with integrity. Now I am running to make sure our state government does the same by protecting taxpayers, defending freedom, and keeping Cherokee County the best place to live, work, and raise a family. I was born and raised here in Cherokee County, this is home. I’ve served as an FBI agent, a prosecutor, and a community leader, and I know how to bring people together to solve problems. I’m not a politician, I’m a public servant and consensus builder.”

ga-house-24-2026-r-1.name: Carter Barrett
ga-house-24-2026-r-1.bio: arter has served his community and Forsyth County on numerous boards and organizations for over 30 years — including Sawnee Community Center, Northside Hospital Foundation, United Way of Forsyth County, GNFCC, Forsyth County Chamber, The Place of Forsyth, Peachtree Parkway Improvement District and Pups with a Purpose. The Georgia House of Representatives has also recognized Carter for his contributions and service to Forsyth County.

ga-house-25-2026-r-2.bio: Todd Jones is a husband, a dad, and community leader; not a career politician.  He has proudly served the constituents of Georgia House District 25 since January 2017.

ga-house-28-2026-r-1.name: Brent Cox
ga-house-28-2026-r-1.bio: Brent Cox is a family man, coach, and hardworking entrepreneur who has owned, managed, and led business ventures for over 24 years. After attending Milton High School, he graduated from the University of Georgia in 1994. Cox then went on to have a successful career in the food service industry where he designed, engineered, and sold ventilation hood systems to chain restaurants on a global scale for over two decades. Cox has also been involved in remediation work for the state and real estate acquisitions. Now, he serves as the President of Simple Mission in Cumming, a company that serves as a manufacturer’s representative in the food service industry.

ga-house-29-2026-r-2.name: Matt Dubnik
ga-house-29-2026-r-2.bio: Born right here in Gainesville, I am a conservative Republican, small business owner, father, husband and someone who is active in my community. I have had a desire to serve my community since I was a young boy. That’s why my responsibility as a public servant for Gainesville and Hall County is not one I take lightly.

ga-house-30-2026-r-1.name: Derrick McCollum
ga-house-30-2026-r-1.bio: Prior to being elected to the Georgia House of Representatives in 2022, Derrick served his community as a firefighter and EMT for Hall County for seventeen years and Habersham County for two years.  In addition to serving as a Majority Deputy Whip, Derrick currently serves on the following committees: Transportation, Secretary; Natural Resources & Environment; Defense & Veterans Affairs

ga-house-35-2026-d-1.name: Lisa Campbell
ga-house-35-2026-d-1.bio: Lisa Campbell was elected as the first woman to Represent Georgia in House District 35. Donate to her campaign for reelection. Sign up to receive her newsletter and stay in the know about legislative issues in Georgia, Town Hall events, and community activities.

ga-house-39-2026-d-1.name: Terry Cummings
ga-house-39-2026-d-1.bio: I grew up on the principle that America is a “melting pot” of different races, cultures, religions, nationalities working together to realize the “American Dream”. I believe all Georgians deserve the opportunity to achieve the dream. As your next state representative, I will work to: -Preserve the rights of all Georgian’s regardless of race, nationality, religion, or sexual orientation.

ga-house-61-2026-d-2.name: Mekyah McQueen
ga-house-61-2026-d-2.bio:
Mcqueen Portraits.png

Mekyah McQueen is an educator, a fierce advocate for youth and families, and an unwavering problem solver. She is the strongest advocate to represent District 61 in the Georgia State House and fight for our Democratic values - a well-funded public education system, protecting our right to vote, and expanding Medicaid.

ga-house-65-2026-d-1.bio: As your State Representative, I've been committed to advancing policies that strengthen our communities and ensure the safety and well-being of all Georgians. In 2025, we achieved significant tax cut, public safety and education gains. I have been outspoken and out front leading the fight for lower prices and lower taxes.  I co-sponsored successful legislation in Fulton and Coweta counties to lower school property taxes for seniors.  We are actively working on legislation to reel in out-of-control auto insurance rates. My primary focus is to "upskill" District 65 for the economy of the future. I want to make sure our youth have access to the education and vocational training they need to prosper.  We need access to a quality job market and substantial business opportunities, so our families and communities grow. We also need financial security for our seniors so they can age in place and leave a legacy in our communities that we can build upon.

ga-house-66-2026-d-2.bio:
Representative Kimberly Alexander was elected to the Georgia General Assembly in 2012. Representative Alexander serves District 66 which encompasses Douglas County and the Southeast section of Paulding County. District 66 is a newly drawn map and Representative Alexander made history by becoming the first African-American female elected to serve the District in Paulding and the first African-American female that lives in Douglas to be elected and serve in Douglas.

In the Georgia General Assembly, Representative Alexander sits on the following Committees; Code Revision, Intragovernmental Coordination, Motor Vehicle, Banks & Banking and Legislative & Congressional Reapportionment.

ocd-person/cf955c60-cbda-4414-9266-3d8dc.name: Tim Fleming
ocd-person/cf955c60-cbda-4414-9266-3d8dc.bio: Tim Fleming is a proven conservative, small business owner, and a champion for election integrity with a strong record of fighting for Georgia. As former Chief of Staff to Gov. Kemp and Deputy Secretary of State under then Sec. Kemp, Tim took on Stacey Abrams' election schemes, cracked down on voter fraud, and streamlined professional licensing. Now serving in the State House, he continues to lead efforts to secure our elections and cut red tape. As Secretary of State, Tim will keep fighting to protect our elections, simplify licensing, and defend Georgia values.

_comment_duplicates: Duplicate SOS filings - same person under different name formats. Keep the first entry (lower number), remove the later entry.
